steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function can be written as $f(x)=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b=f(0)$.

steps.title: Substitute the values
steps.content: The slope is $\frac{1}{3}$, so $m=\frac{1}{3}$. The line passes through $(0,7)$, so $f(0)=7$ and $b=7$. Therefore $$f(x)=\frac{1}{3}x+7.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: $f(x)=\frac{1}{3}x+7$ matches choice A. The other three choices use the wrong $y$-intercept, so they fail the point $(0,7)$. Choice A is correct.
